info.title: Media Management Collections API
info.description:
Enhanced Collections management API following industry best practices for REST APIs.

## Key Features
- Hierarchical collection structure with unlimited nesting
- Custom collection types configured at system level
- Metadata inheritance from parent collections
- Rules-based automatic item assignment
- Batch operations for efficient item management
- Granular permission management
- Collection sharing and collaboration features
- Cursor-based pagination for optimal performance

## Response Format
All responses follow a consistent structure with success/error indicators,
data payload, pagination info (when applicable), and metadata.

parameters.name: filter[type]
parameters.description: Filter by collection type (e.g., project, task)

parameters.name: filter[search]
parameters.description: Search collections by name or metadata

parameters.name: filter[parentId]
parameters.description: Filter by parent collection ID (null for root collections)

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of related resources to include

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return

parameters.name: cascade
parameters.description: Delete child collections and remove items

400.description: Cannot delete collection with children (when cascade=false)

post.summary: Add a single item to collection
Add multiple items to collection (batch operation)
Remove multiple items from collection (batch operation)

get.summary: List rules for automatic item assignment

post.summary: Add a new rule for automatic item assignment

get.summary: List all users and groups with access

post.summary: Share collection with users or groups

get.summary: List collections shared with current user

priority.description: Higher priority rules are evaluated first

matchCount.description: Number of items currently matching this rule

role.description:
Permission levels:
- viewer: Read-only access
- editor: Add/remove items and modify metadata
- admin: Manage permissions and settings
